title: quercus × urartensis
intro: Quercus × urartensis, also known as the Turkish oak, is a hybrid of Quercus robur and Quercus petraea, and it is native to the mountainous regions of Turkey. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus × urartensis include the potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
